channel.title: Hoops Intel — Daily NBA Scores & Analysis
channel.description: Your daily NBA intelligence briefing with scores, player rankings, injury reports, and game previews.

item.title: Knueppel Makes History — Youngest to 250 Threes · Banchero 30 Snaps Magic Skid · Duren 30/10/7 Without Cunningham
item.description: Kon Knueppel scores 26 with 6 three-pointers to become the youngest player in NBA history with 250 threes in a season; Banchero's 30/9/7 ends Orlando's 6-game losing streak; Duren dominates as Pistons cruise without Cunningham — Kon Knueppel wrote himself into the NBA record books. The 20-year-old scored 26 points with six three-pointers — becoming the youngest player in NBA history to hit 250 threes in a single season — while nearly posting a triple-double (11 rebounds, 8 assists) as the Hornets dismantled the Knicks 114-103, snapping New York's 7-game winning streak. Charlotte (39-34) has won five straight. Jalen Duren posted 30/10/7 on 10-of-12 shooting as the Pistons beat the Pelicans 129-108 — their third win in four games without Cade Cunningham (collapsed lung). Paolo Banchero had 30/9/7 as the Magic ended a six-game skid with a 121-117 win over Sacramento. Three teams — Orlando, Charlotte, and Miami — are now tied at 39-34 in the East play-in race.

item.title: Celtics End OKC's 12-Game Streak · Embiid & George Return With 63 Combined · Jokic's Historic 23/21/19 · Luka 43 · Hawks OT Upset in Detroit
item.description: Jaylen Brown scores 31 as Boston ends Oklahoma City's 12-game winning streak 119-109; Joel Embiid (35) and Paul George (28) return together as Sixers score 157; Jokić nearly records first-ever 20-20-20 game with 23/21/19; Luka drops 43 on Indiana; Hawks stun Pistons in OT — The night the streaks died. Jaylen Brown scored 31 points — 14 in a pivotal third quarter — as the Celtics rallied from 13 down to beat Oklahoma City 119-109, snapping the Thunder's 12-game winning streak. Jayson Tatum added 19 and 12 rebounds. Joel Embiid returned from injury with 35 points and Paul George had 28 in his first game back from a 25-game suspension as the Sixers scored 157 against Chicago — their highest-scoring game of the season. Nikola Jokic had one of the most absurd stat lines in NBA history — 23 points, 21 rebounds, 19 assists — falling one assist short of the first 20-20-20 game in NBA history as Denver beat Dallas 142-135. Luka Doncic dropped 43 on 15-of-30 shooting as the Lakers beat Indiana 137-130. CJ McCollum scored 27 with the tiebreaking 3-point play in OT as the Hawks stunned the Pistons 130-129 — Atlanta's 14th win in 15 games. Minnesota edged Houston 110-108 in overtime. Miami topped Cleveland 120-103.

item.title: SGA Extends Streak to 131 · Jokić 23/17/17 Edges Suns · Knicks Snap Suns' Home Streak · Thunder W12 · Spurs Cruise
item.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander pushes his consecutive 20-point game record to 131 as OKC wins their 12th straight; Jokic posts 23/17/17 in Denver's 125-123 win at Phoenix; Brunson's 27 leads Knicks past Suns; Spurs extend their win streak to six — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander extended his all-time record to 131 consecutive 20-point games as the Thunder won their 12th straight. The MVP frontrunner continues to set records that may never be broken. Nikola Jokic had another absurd triple-double — 23 points, 17 rebounds, 17 assists — as Denver edged Phoenix 125-123 on the road. The Suns dropped to 40-32 and continue to slide. Jalen Brunson scored 27 as the Knicks beat the Suns 119-112 — a result that further complicates Phoenix's postseason positioning. The Spurs cruised to their sixth straight win behind Wembanyama's all-around excellence. Luka Doncic had 32 points in a 113-110 loss at Detroit on the previous night, as the Lakers' road trip hit a bump.

item.title: Luka's 38 Powers Lakers Past Rockets · Hawks Win 11th Straight · SGA's 130th Consecutive 20+ Game · Tatum 31 Routs Warriors
item.description: Luka Dončić scores 38 with a clutch step-back three as the Lakers beat Houston 118-115 for their seventh straight win; Hawks beat Dallas for their 11th consecutive victory; SGA cruises to 25 in three quarters as OKC demolishes Brooklyn; Tatum drops 31 as Celtics handle Curry-less Warriors — Luka Dončić scored 38 points on 13-of-24 shooting — including a step-back three with 47 seconds left — as the Lakers beat the Rockets 118-115 in Houston for their seventh consecutive win. Anthony Davis added 24 and 13 rebounds. SGA extended his record to 130 consecutive 20-point games with 25 in three quarters as OKC rolled Brooklyn 121-98 for their 11th straight. The Hawks won their 11th straight behind Jalen Johnson's 28/9/7 in a 122-116 win in Dallas — Cooper Flagg had 25/8 in defeat. Tatum scored 31 as Boston crushed Golden State 118-101 — the Warriors dropped below .500. Portland beat Indiana 116-105 behind Avdija's 22. Toronto topped Chicago 119-111 as Ingram scored 28 for the third straight game. Minnesota cruised past Utah 120-104 without Edwards. The Clippers survived in New Orleans 112-106 with Kawhi on a minutes restriction. Denver beat Memphis 119-105 as Jokic had 22/11/9.

item.title: Luka's 38 Powers Lakers Past Rockets · Hawks Win 11th Straight · SGA's 130th Consecutive 20+ Game · Tatum 31 Routs Warriors
item.description: Luka Dončić scores 38 with a clutch step-back three as the Lakers beat Houston 118-115 for their seventh straight win; Hawks beat Dallas for their 11th consecutive victory; SGA cruises to 25 in three quarters as OKC demolishes Brooklyn; Tatum drops 31 as Celtics handle Curry-less Warriors — Luka Dončić scored 38 points on 13-of-24 shooting — including a step-back three with 47 seconds left — as the Lakers beat the Rockets 118-115 in Houston for their seventh consecutive win. Anthony Davis added 24 and 13 rebounds. SGA extended his record to 130 consecutive 20-point games with 25 in three quarters as OKC rolled Brooklyn 121-98 for their 11th straight. The Hawks won their 11th straight behind Jalen Johnson's 28/9/7 in a 122-116 win in Dallas — Cooper Flagg had 25/8 in defeat. Tatum scored 31 as Boston crushed Golden State 118-101 — the Warriors dropped below .500. Portland beat Indiana 116-105 behind Avdija's 22. Toronto topped Chicago 119-111 as Ingram scored 28 for the third straight game. Minnesota cruised past Utah 120-104 without Edwards. The Clippers survived in New Orleans 112-106 with Kawhi on a minutes restriction. Denver beat Memphis 119-105 as Jokic had 22/11/9.

item.title: Wembanyama 33/11/5 BLK Dismantles Suns · Cunningham Returns for 24 · Banchero Outduels Mobley · Luka 36 Leads Lakers Rout
item.description: Victor Wembanyama posts 33 points, 11 rebounds, and 5 blocks as the Spurs rout Phoenix 129-108; Cade Cunningham returns from back spasms with 24 points and 8 assists; Banchero edges Mobley in Orlando; Luka drops 36 in a home blowout of Sacramento — Victor Wembanyama was a force of nature in San Antonio — 33 points, 11 rebounds, and 5 blocks as the Spurs routed Phoenix 129-108. De'Aaron Fox added 28 points and the Spurs (52-18) remain the West's second-best team. Kevin Durant scored 26 in the loss but the Suns fell to .500 in their last 10. The injury story of the night was a good one: Cade Cunningham made a triumphant return from his back spasms, posting 24 points, 8 assists, and 5 rebounds as Detroit beat New Orleans 121-103. Duren added 18 and 10. Cade looked healthy and explosive. Paolo Banchero had 27 points and 9 rebounds as Orlando topped Cleveland 118-113 — Evan Mobley countered with 25 and 12. Luka Doncic dropped 36 with 9 rebounds and 8 assists as the Lakers blew out Sacramento 131-109 at Crypto.com Arena — Austin Reaves added 24. Brooklyn upset Chicago 109-101 behind Nic Claxton's 22 and 12 to snap a 4-game skid.

item.title: SGA Extends Streak to 130 · Thunder 11 Straight · Luka 35 Edges Houston Thriller · Hawks Make It 11 · Jokic Triple-Double
item.description: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 27 before resting in the fourth as OKC rolls Brooklyn for their 11th consecutive win; Luka ices a road thriller in Houston with a pull-up three with 18 seconds left; Atlanta extends their run to 11 straight games — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander extended his all-time record to 130 consecutive 20-point games — and barely broke a sweat doing it. SGA scored 27 in three quarters, then sat the fourth as OKC routed Brooklyn 119-97 for their 11th consecutive win. The Thunder (55-15) are the undisputed class of the NBA. Luka Doncic was brilliant in Houston — 35 points, 8 rebounds, and 10 assists — as the Lakers held off the Rockets 120-117 on the road, iced by Luka's pull-up three with 18 seconds left. Los Angeles (44-25) has now won seven straight. The Celtics handled the Curry-less Warriors 121-104 in Boston — Jayson Tatum had 24 and Jaylen Brown added 21. Nikola Jokic posted his 28th triple-double of the season (19 points, 14 rebounds, 11 assists) as Denver beat Memphis 116-104. The Hawks made it 11 straight — Nickeil Alexander-Walker had 25 as Atlanta escaped Dallas 121-115 despite Cooper Flagg's 26 points, 9 rebounds, and 6 assists. Minnesota beat Utah 122-101 without Edwards — Randle had 28 and Hyland 24 off the bench. Brandon Ingram dropped 30 as Toronto beat Chicago 115-108. The Clippers held the 8-seed with a 110-103 win in New Orleans — Kawhi played 24 limited minutes and scored 22. Portland beat Indiana 114-103 behind Deni Avdija's 24 points.

item.title: SGA's 40 Pushes Streak to 129 · Cunningham Back Spasms Shake East · Hart 12-for-13 Without Brunson · LaMelo 30/13 · Edwards Out Weeks
item.description: Gilgeous-Alexander drops 40 as Thunder win tenth straight; Cunningham exits after 5 minutes with back spasms; Edwards diagnosed with knee inflammation — out 1-2 weeks; Hart goes 12-for-13 as Knicks blow out Indiana without Brunson; LaMelo Ball's 30/13 demolishes Heat in Charlotte's biggest win of the season — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander dropped 40 points on 14-of-27 shooting to extend his all-time record to 129 consecutive 20-point games as the Thunder beat the Magic 113-108 for their tenth straight win. Cade Cunningham exited after just five minutes with back spasms in Detroit's 130-117 win over Washington — Jalen Duren had a career-high 36 points. Anthony Edwards was diagnosed with right knee inflammation and will miss 1-2 weeks. Giannis Antetokounmpo was officially diagnosed with a bone bruise and won't be re-evaluated until March 28. Josh Hart went 12-for-13 (5-5 3PT) for 33 points as the Knicks crushed Indiana 136-110 without Brunson. LaMelo Ball had 30 and 13 assists as Charlotte blew out Miami 136-106. Evan Mobley posted 27/15 as Cleveland beat the Bucks 123-116. The Spurs rolled Sacramento 132-104 behind Wemby's 18/8.

item.title: Hawks Make It 10 Straight — Magic Streak Ends at 7 · Giannis MRI: Bone Bruise Confirmed, Out Until March 28 · Curry Diagnosed With Runner's Knee · Cunningham 27 Leads Pistons
item.description: Atlanta's Alexander-Walker drops 28 to end Orlando's 7-game run and push the Hawks to 10 consecutive wins; Antetokounmpo officially diagnosed with bone bruise on hyperextended left knee; Golden State confirms Curry out indefinitely with bilateral runner's knee — The Atlanta Hawks made it 10 consecutive wins — beating the Orlando Magic 108-102 to end Orlando's own 7-game win streak in the process. Nickeil Alexander-Walker dropped 28 points with 5-of-9 from three and Jalen Johnson had 21 points and 11 rebounds. A light Monday slate of four games was dominated by injury news. Giannis Antetokounmpo's MRI results were officially released: bone bruise on the hyperextended left knee — he will not be re-evaluated until March 28 and will miss at least his next five games. In Milwaukee's 116-104 win over Philadelphia, Kevin Porter Jr. stepped up with 26 points and 10 assists as Milwaukee's primary creator. Golden State separately announced Stephen Curry has been diagnosed with bilateral runner's knee with no return date given — casting serious doubt over the Warriors' play-in chances. Cade Cunningham bounced back emphatically from his early exit against Washington — 27 points and 8 assists as Detroit beat Houston 118-109. Jalen Duren added 21 points and 10 rebounds. The Pistons (49-18) maintained the East's best record. Brandon Ingram scored 31 as Toronto beat Memphis 119-107 — the Grizzlies fell to their seventh consecutive loss, and Ja Morant remains without a return timetable.

item.title: SGA Barely Survives — 128 Straight · Giannis Hyperextends Knee · DeRozan 41/11 · Flagg's Revenge · Brunson's 21-Point Comeback
item.description: Gilgeous-Alexander scores exactly 20 to extend record to 128 games; Antetokounmpo hyperextends knee in Bucks win; Brunson leads Knicks back from 21 down on ABC; Flagg drops 27/10 in Cleveland revenge game — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored exactly 20 points with 10 assists — his closest call — to extend his record to 128 consecutive 20-point games as OKC beat Minnesota 116-103. Giannis Antetokounmpo scored 31/14/8 before hyperextending his left knee on a dunk — MRI pending — as the Bucks beat Indiana 134-123. Brunson scored 30 with 9 assists (10-10 FT) to lead the Knicks back from 21 down to beat the Warriors 110-107 on ABC. Cooper Flagg had 27/10/6 as Dallas got revenge on Cleveland 130-120, two days after a 33-point loss in the same building. DeMar DeRozan posted a season-high 41/11 as the Kings beat Utah 116-111. Brandon Ingram scored 34 as Toronto beat East-leading Detroit 119-108. Quentin Grimes had 31 as the shorthanded Sixers beat Portland 109-103.

item.title: Luka's OT Dagger Beats Nuggets · Reaves' Free-Throw Trick · Hawks Win 9th Straight · Wemby Returns With 32/12/8
item.description: Doncic hits game-winning jumper with 0.5 seconds left in OT as Lakers beat Nuggets 127-125; Reaves forces overtime by rebounding his intentionally missed FT; McCollum passes Pierce; Banchero beats Heat for 5th time — Luka Doncic hit a fall-away jumper with 0.5 seconds left in overtime as the Lakers beat the Nuggets 127-125 on ABC. Austin Reaves forced OT by intentionally missing a free throw, grabbing the rebound, and hitting a floater with 1.9 seconds left. Luka had 30/11/13 and Reaves added 32. Jokic had 24/16/14 but Murray went 1-14 and fouled out 31 seconds into OT. CJ McCollum scored 30 with 7-10 from three — surpassing Paul Pierce for 16th all-time in 3PM — as the Hawks beat the Bucks 122-99 for their 9th straight win. Wembanyama returned with 32/12/8/3BLK as the Spurs beat the Hornets 115-102. Banchero had 27/8/7 as the Magic beat the Heat 121-117 for their 7th straight (5-0 vs MIA). Tatum had 20/14 as the Celtics beat the Wizards 111-100. The Kings shocked the Clippers 118-109 behind Westbrook's 209th triple-double.

item.title: Edwards Erupts for 42 · Mobley's 29 Destroys Dallas · Duren 30/13 · Ingram's 36-Point Comeback · Brunson 29
item.description: Anthony Edwards scores 42 as Timberwolves hold off Warriors 127-117; Evan Mobley matches season-high 29 (12-15 FG) as Cavaliers crush Mavericks 138-105; Pistons hand Grizzlies 7th straight loss — Anthony Edwards scored 42 points (13-22 FG, 12-12 FT) with 8 rebounds and 5 assists as the Timberwolves beat the Warriors 127-117. Evan Mobley had 29 points on 12-15 shooting as the Cavaliers demolished Dallas 138-105 — Cooper Flagg scored 25 in the loss. Jalen Brunson scored 29 and Mitchell Robinson had a career-high 22 rebounds as the Knicks beat Indiana 101-92 — the Pacers' 12th straight loss. Jalen Duren had 30 points and 13 rebounds as the Pistons beat Memphis 126-110 for the Grizzlies' 7th straight loss. Brandon Ingram scored 36 as the Raptors rallied to beat the Suns 122-115. Kevin Durant's 18-footer with 7.6 seconds left lifted Houston over New Orleans 107-105. Kawhi scored 28 as the Clippers beat the Bulls 119-108. Portland beat Utah 124-114 behind Henderson, Holiday, and Clingan.

item.title: SGA Breaks Wilt's Record — 127 Games · Luka Career-High 51 · Jokic 31/20/12 Monster Triple-Double
item.description: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 35 to break Chamberlain's 63-year-old record of consecutive 20-point games; Doncic erupts for 51 as Lakers torch Bulls 142-130; Jokic rallies Denver from 20 down — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 35 points to break Wilt Chamberlain's 63-year-old record with his 127th consecutive 20-point game as OKC edged Boston 104-102. Luka Doncic posted a career-high 51 points as the Lakers torched the Bulls 142-130. Nikola Jokic had 31/20/12 and Jamal Murray added 39 as Denver rallied from 20 down to beat San Antonio 136-131. Bam Adebayo scored 21 and Larsson added 28 as Miami won its 7th straight over Milwaukee 112-105. Devin Booker scored 43 and Jeff Green had 36 in Phoenix's 123-108 win over Indiana. Jalen Suggs scored 28 as Orlando won its 6th straight in OT over Washington 136-131. Khris Middleton scored 35 (22 in Q4) as Dallas snapped an 8-game losing streak over Memphis 120-112.

item.title: Kawhi Explodes for 45 · Jokic Triple-Doubles Denver to a Blowout · Knicks Rally From 18 Down · Magic Win 5th Straight · DeRozan Passes Duncan
item.description: Kawhi Leonard scores 45 on 15-20 FG as Clippers destroy Minnesota 153-128; Jokic 16/13/12 in 30 min as Denver crushes Houston 129-93; Brunson leads NYK comeback; Banchero beats Cleveland; LaMelo 30 in Charlotte road win — Kawhi Leonard scored 45 points on 15-20 shooting as the Clippers routed the Timberwolves 153-128 for their highest-scoring game of the season. Nikola Jokic posted his 25th triple-double (16/13/12) as Denver blew out Houston 129-93 — winning the season series 3-1. Jalen Brunson scored 28 and led a comeback from 18 down as the Knicks beat the Jazz 134-117. Paolo Banchero had 25/8/7 as Orlando beat Cleveland 128-122 for their 5th straight win. Trey Murphy scored 28 as the Pelicans beat the Raptors 122-111 — Toronto dropped to 7th in the East. LaMelo Ball scored 30 and Kon Knueppel added 24 as Charlotte beat Sacramento 117-109 — DeMar DeRozan passed Tim Duncan for 18th all-time with 39.

item.title: BAM. 83 Points. Second-Best Ever. · Wemby 39/11/8 Threes Sinks Celtics · Luka Triple-Double Sweeps Wolves · Dallas Loses 8th Straight
item.description: Bam Adebayo scores 83 points — 2nd-most in NBA history behind Wilt's 100 — with NBA-record 36 free throws; Wembanyama career-high 8 threes; Lakers leap Timberwolves; Spurs 48-17 — Bam Adebayo scored 83 points — the second-most in NBA history behind Wilt Chamberlain's 100 — shooting 20-43 with an NBA-record 36-43 FT as Miami beat Washington 150-129. Wembanyama had 39/11 with 8 threes as the Spurs beat the Celtics 125-116 on NBC — Brown was ejected. Luka posted a 31/11/11 triple-double and Reaves had 31 (7 3PM) as the Lakers swept Minnesota 120-106. Cunningham had 21/15 as Detroit crushed Brooklyn 138-100. The Hawks won their 7th straight beating Dallas 124-112. Cam Payne scored 32 as the Sixers beat Memphis 139-129. Durant had 29 as Houston beat Toronto 113-99.

item.title: SGA Ties Wilt. Game-Winner. History. · Harden Reaches 29,000 · KAT 35/12/7 in Knicks Loss · Warriors Sink to .500
item.description: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 35 with career-high 15 assists and zero turnovers, then drills step-back 3 with 2.7 seconds left to tie Wilt Chamberlain's 126-game record and beat Denver 129-126 — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander tied Wilt Chamberlain's 63-year-old record of 126 consecutive 20-point games with a 35-point, 15-assist, 0-turnover masterclass capped by a step-back three at the buzzer to beat Denver 129-126 — without four starters. James Harden became the 9th player to reach 29,000 career points as Cleveland beat Philadelphia 115-101. Karl-Anthony Towns had 35/12/7 on 76% shooting but the Knicks lost to the Clippers 126-118 as Mikal Bridges went scoreless. The Jazz upset Golden State 119-116 as the Warriors fell to .500. Brooklyn beat Memphis 126-115.

item.title: Tatum Owns Cleveland · Luka's Triple-Double · Wemby 34/14/6BLK Buries Houston · Dallas Loses 7th Straight
item.description: Tatum drops 28/9/6 on the road in game three of Achilles return; Luka 38/12/10 edges Brunson 33; Wembanyama pushes Spurs to 15-1 since Feb 1; Banchero outduels Giannis — Jayson Tatum scored 28/9/6 in 32 minutes in his first road game back from the Achilles as Boston beat Cleveland 112-102. Luka Doncic posted a 38/12/10 triple-double to edge Brunson's 33/11 in a 127-124 Lakers win on ABC. Victor Wembanyama went 34/14/6BLK as the Spurs crushed Houston 119-108 on NBC — San Antonio is 15-1 since Feb 1. Tyler Herro scored 31 as Miami beat Detroit 105-99. Paolo Banchero had 28/11 as Orlando beat Giannis and Milwaukee 118-112 for their 4th straight win. Dallas lost its 7th straight in Toronto despite Flagg's 21/9/5.

item.title: SGA One From Wilt · Thunder First to 50 · Banchero 25/15 Destroys Minnesota · Nets Snap 10-Game Skid
item.description: Gilgeous-Alexander extends 20-point streak to 125 — one shy of Chamberlain's 63-year-old record; OKC becomes first team to 50 wins — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 27 points (14-15 FT) as the Thunder beat the Warriors 104-97 to become the first team to 50 wins. SGA extended his streak to 125 consecutive 20-point games — one shy of Wilt Chamberlain's record. Paolo Banchero posted 25/15 and Desmond Bane scored 30 as Orlando crushed Minnesota 119-92. The Nets snapped a 10-game losing streak in Detroit 107-105.

item.title: 298 Days. Tatum Is Back — Luka 44, Wemby's 25-Point Comeback, Murray's Ankle
item.description: Tatum returns from Achilles with 15/12/7; Luka drops 44 (22 in Q1); Wembanyama leads SAS from 25 down in 22 minutes — Jayson Tatum returned from a torn Achilles (298 days) with 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists in Boston's 120-100 win over Dallas. Luka Doncic scored 22 points in Q1 alone and finished with 44 in a Lakers win without LeBron and Davis. Victor Wembanyama put up 27/10/4BLK in 22 minutes to lead San Antonio from 25 points down to beat the Clippers. Jamal Murray sprained his ankle in the Knicks' 39-point demolition of Denver — MRI results pending.

item.title: Flagg Returns to 115-114 Heartbreak · Podziemski's OT Heroics · LeBron Passes Kareem
item.description: Cooper Flagg's comeback spoiled by Wendell Carter Jr.'s buzzer dunk; Warriors outlast Rockets in OT; LeBron makes history in Denver loss — Cooper Flagg returned from his midfoot sprain only to watch Wendell Carter Jr. dunk with 1.4 seconds left to give Orlando a 115-114 win. Brandon Podziemski scored 26 points as the short-handed Warriors beat Houston 115-113 in OT. LeBron James passed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar for the most made field goals in NBA history — then lost to Denver 120-113. Wembanyama scored 38 against Detroit as the Spurs rolled. Collin Sexton hit 30 to upset Phoenix. Zion had 23 as New Orleans routed Sacramento.

item.title: Hornets Stun Celtics 118-89 · SGA Edges Knicks in MSG Thriller · Keyonte George Drops 30
item.description: Charlotte extends win streak to six with a 29-point rout of Boston; Thunder survive the Garden 103-100 — Kon Knueppel scored 20 points as the Charlotte Hornets demolished Boston 118-89, extending their win streak to six games. Derrick White scored 29 for the Celtics in a losing effort. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander led OKC past the Knicks 103-100 at MSG with 26 points and 8 assists — Chet Holmgren had 14 in the first quarter. Keyonte George scored 30 in Utah's 102-106 loss to Philadelphia.

item.title: Edwards Erupts for 41 · Spurs Rout Sixers by 40 · Brunson's 26 Buries Toronto
item.description: Anthony Edwards joins rare Wolves history with 41 points and 5 steals; Wembanyama's Spurs obliterate Philly 131-91 — Anthony Edwards scored 41 points with 5 steals — becoming the third Timberwolves player ever with 40+ points and 5+ steals — in a 117-110 win over Memphis. The Spurs demolished the 76ers 131-91 as Dylan Harper and Devin Vassell each scored 22 while Wembanyama cruised with 10 points in limited minutes. Philadelphia became the first team in NBA history to lose three home games by 40+ points in a single season. Jalen Brunson scored 26 in the Knicks' 111-95 win in Toronto. Luka had 27 as the Lakers beat the Pelicans.

item.title: Murray's 45-Point Thriller · Celtics Spoil Giannis Return · Trae Young Ejected Before Debut
item.description: Murray drops 45 on 68% shooting in a 3-point Denver survival; Boston wins 108-81 without Tatum or Brown — Jamal Murray scored 45 points on 13-of-19 shooting with eight three-pointers, making the go-ahead free throws with 31.8 seconds left to lift Denver past Utah 128-125. Meanwhile, the Celtics dominated the Giannis return game 108-81 without Tatum or Brown, and Trae Young was ejected before playing a single minute for Washington.

item.title: Giddey Ties Pippen · Jokic's 35/13/9 in a Loss · Spurs' Streak Snapped
item.description: Chicago's 27-0 fourth-quarter run ends 11-game skid; Knicks hold Spurs to season-low 89 — Josh Giddey's triple-double and a 27-0 fourth-quarter run ended Chicago's 11-game losing streak, while the Knicks snapped San Antonio's 11-game winning streak at MSG.

item.title: Wemby Carries Spurs to 11th Straight · Luka Drops 40 · Celtics Roll
item.description: San Antonio completes an undefeated February; Doncic goes off in Lakers win — Victor Wembanyama led San Antonio to their 11th consecutive win, completing an undefeated February for the Spurs. Luka Doncic dropped 40 in a Lakers blowout.

item.title: SGA Returns · Thunder Extend Lead · Flagg Sidelined
item.description: Oklahoma City's star guard back from abdominal strain; Cooper Flagg's midfoot injury confirmed — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander returned from a two-week absence with 28 points as OKC extended their lead atop the Western Conference. Cooper Flagg's midfoot sprain diagnosis puts his ROY campaign in jeopardy.

item.title: Knueppel Erupts for 38 · Hornets Surge · Tatum Rehab Update
item.description: Charlotte's rookie sensation posts career-high as Hornets win fifth straight — Kon Knueppel posted a career-high 38 points as the Charlotte Hornets won their fifth consecutive game, one of the league's most surprising runs. Jayson Tatum's Achilles rehab is reportedly ahead of schedule.

item.title: Jokic's 50-Point Night · Nuggets Snap Skid · Edwards Ejected
item.description: Nikola Jokic reaches 50 for the first time this season in a statement win over Phoenix — Nikola Jokic scored 50 points — his first 50-point game of the season — as Denver snapped a three-game losing streak with a dominant win over Phoenix. Anthony Edwards was ejected in the third quarter of Minnesota's loss.

item.title: Celtics Win 12th Straight · Queta Dominates · Brown's 40-Point Night
item.description: Boston's winning streak reaches 12 as Jaylen Brown goes off for 40 without Tatum — The Boston Celtics won their 12th consecutive game as Jaylen Brown scored 40 points and Neemias Queta posted another double-double. The Celtics are the hottest team in the East.

item.title: All-Star Hangover · Luka's Return · West Shakeup
item.description: First games back from the All-Star break deliver drama across the league — The first night back from the All-Star break delivered drama across the league. Luka Doncic returned to the Lakers lineup with 32 points, while the Western Conference standings tightened with multiple upsets.
